payload.summary: The branch correctly fixes the core issue: artifact metadata now reflects the actual backend/model from InvocationEnvelope rather than the pre-configured resolved target. The three cleanup amendments (eprintln removal, debug-file removal, result symlink exclusion) are straightforward hygiene. The two structural amendments proposing a new invocation_metadata field and corresponding test changes are over-engineering — the existing RecordProducer::Agent variant already carries backend_family and model_id correctly after the fix, making a redundant field unnecessary.

votes.rationale: The two eprintln! blocks are unconditional, use raw stderr instead of the project's structured tracing infrastructure, and are unrelated to the artifact-metadata fix. They dump potentially large stdout/stderr payloads on every parse failure. Removing them is correct cleanup; if parse-failure diagnostics are later needed, they should go through tracing with appropriate log levels.

The unconditional --debug-file argument generates a debug log file in runtime/temp/ for every single Claude invocation. This is clearly debugging scaffolding — it has no gating (no feature flag, no config toggle, no log-level check) and would create unbounded disk usage in production. It is unrelated to the artifact-metadata fix and should be removed.

The result symlink is a Nix build artifact pointing to a local store path. It is environment-specific, non-reproducible across machines, and should not be version-controlled. It should be excluded from the branch (and ideally added to .gitignore if not already present).

The existing RecordProducer::Agent { backend_family, model_id } already carries exactly the metadata this amendment proposes to duplicate into a separate invocation_metadata field. After the fix, producer fields correctly reflect envelope.metadata.backend_used.family and envelope.metadata.model_used.model_id — verified by both the test code and persisted artifact JSON on disk. Adding a parallel invocation_metadata struct would create redundant data, increase serialization surface area, and introduce a maintenance burden to keep both representations in sync — all for no additional information. The producer field is the semantic home for 'what produced this artifact' metadata.

This amendment's primary ask — asserting invocation_metadata.backend_family and invocation_metadata.model_id — depends on the rejected invocation_metadata field from fr-2-748d0beb. Without that field, there is nothing to assert. The existing test in final_review.rs (persisted_artifacts_use_envelope_metadata_not_resolved_target) already verifies that 4+ agent-produced artifacts carry the correct producer.backend_family and producer.model_id from envelope metadata rather than configured targets. The service.rs test additionally verifies that InvocationEnvelope metadata is preserved during invocation. Together these provide adequate regression coverage for the fix.
